title: Cellular site monitoring systems and methods
abstract: A computer-implemented method for improved monitoring of cellular sites. The method includes identifying a base station of the one or more base stations. The base station has a base station status that is a down status and, based on the down status, the method includes identifying whether a connection is functioning between a mediation server associated with the base station and an operational support system (OSS) associated with an OSS database. The method includes determining whether the base station is connected to the mediation server and, when the base station is connected to the mediation server and the connection is functioning between the mediation server and the OSS, updating the base station status to be an up status.
claims: 1. A computer-implemented method for improved monitoring of cellular sites, the method comprising: identifying a first base station of the one or more base stations, the first base station having a base station status that is a down status; based on the down status, identifying whether a connection is functioning between a mediation server associated with the first base station and an operational support system (OSS) associated with an OSS database; determining whether the first base station is connected to the mediation server; when the first base station is connected to the mediation server and the connection is functioning between the mediation server and the OSS, updating the first base station status to be an up status; identifying a second base station of the one or more base stations, the second base station having an up status; determining that the second base station is not connected to a mediation server associated with the second base station; and based on the determination that the second base station is not connected to the mediation server associated with the second base station, updating the second base station status to be a down status.
